body.title: [sprint] proof_emitter: accept a symbol-stub / new-module as authentic red
body.intention: I intend that a new-module increment can earn a real commit-bound proof, so honest work stops closing shipped-unproven for a mere proof-binding-convention gap.
body.description:
**Problem (recurring — bit T-router-data-schema and T-router-live-resource-read):** proof_emitter requires the red state to fail on an ASSERTION about behavior. Two common, honest patterns can't produce that today, so they close shipped-unproven for a convention gap, NOT a real missing proof: (1) test+impl committed together (no red state in history); (2) a NEW MODULE — the test-only red stub fails on ModuleNotFoundError (CollectionError), which proof_emitter rejects as 'collateral error, not an assertion'. Both are proof-BINDING gaps, not missing-proof: the mechanism is fully tested green.
**Proposed shape:** add a proof_emitter mode that synthesizes authentic assertion-red from a SINGLE green commit by reverting only the NON-TEST files touched by the commit to their parent state (or, for a new module, temporarily replacing it with a symbol-stub that imports but returns placeholder values), running the named test → expecting an assertion failure → then restoring and confirming green. This binds a real red→green proof to one commit without requiring stub-first commit ordering. Guard: refuse if reverting non-test files still leaves the test green (vacuous) or if it errors on collection (distinguish 'module missing entirely' from 'symbol-stub present').
**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/proof_emitter.py.
**Design rules:** proof must stay commit-bound and honest (no green-against-dirty-tree); NO SQLite.
**Scope boundary:** proof_emitter red-synthesis only. Does not change the proof-on-close gate or cc_queue close.
**Test plan:** (1) a single commit adding a new module + its passing test → proof_emitter emits a proof (red synthesized by symbol-stub, green restored); (2) a vacuous test (passes even with non-test files reverted) → NO PROOF; (3) existing stub-first path (separate red commit) still works.
**Completion criteria:** proof_emitter can emit an authentic red->green proof from a single test+impl commit (incl. a new module) without manual stub-first ordering; vacuous tests still rejected.
